description:
You are acting as an MLOps engineer who wants to keep very lightweight, self-contained records of model-training experiments.  
    Complete the following steps **inside the Linux terminal**.  
    All work must stay under the directory **/home/user/mlops/** (create it if it does not yet exist).

    1. Create a new SQLite database file called **experiments.db** in **/home/user/mlops/**.  
    2. Inside that database, create a single table **experiment_runs** with exactly these columns (name ‑> type):  
       • run_id ‑> INTEGER PRIMARY KEY  
       • model  ‑> TEXT  
       • accuracy ‑> REAL  
       • timestamp ‑> TEXT  
    3. Insert one record into this table with the following values *in the listed order*:  
       1, "resnet50", 0.8731, "2023-10-12T14:32:00Z"
    4. Verify the insertion by selecting the row back and then export the query result to a CSV file named **run_1_summary.csv** in **/home/user/mlops/artifacts/** (create the **artifacts** directory if necessary).

       Required CSV format:  
       • The file **/home/user/mlops/artifacts/run_1_summary.csv** must contain exactly two lines.  
         Line 1 (header): `run_id,model,accuracy,timestamp`  
         Line 2 (data)  : `1,resnet50,0.8731,2023-10-12T14:32:00Z`  
       • Use plain commas, no additional spaces or quotes around fields.  
       • Make sure the file ends with a single trailing newline character (`\n`).

    5. For traceability, write a simple text log file **/home/user/mlops/sql_commands.log** containing **exactly** the raw SQL statements you executed (one per line, in execution order, each ending with a semicolon).  
       The file must therefore have precisely these two lines and nothing else:  
       ```
       CREATE TABLE experiment_runs (run_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, model TEXT, accuracy REAL, timestamp TEXT);
       INSERT INTO experiment_runs (run_id, model, accuracy, timestamp) VALUES (1,'resnet50',0.8731,'2023-10-12T14:32:00Z');
       ```

    What the grader will check:
    • The existence of the directory **/home/user/mlops/**.  
    • The SQLite file **/home/user/mlops/experiments.db** exists and contains table **experiment_runs** with the defined schema.  
    • The table holds exactly one row whose values match the ones given above.  
    • The CSV file **/home/user/mlops/artifacts/run_1_summary.csv** matches the required two-line format *byte-for-byte*.  
    • The log file **/home/user/mlops/sql_commands.log** contains the two SQL statements on two separate lines, nothing more, nothing less.

    Hint: You can use the `sqlite3` CLI (already installed in most base images) and its `.mode csv` / `.headers on` functionality to generate the CSV, or redirect output manually—choose any method you like as long as the final files meet the specifications.
truth:
Directory that must exist before verification:
      /home/user/mlops/

    Files and required exact contents after the agent finishes:

    1. /home/user/mlops/experiments.db  
       • Must contain a table named "experiment_runs" with columns:
         run_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, model TEXT, accuracy REAL, timestamp TEXT  
       • Table must have exactly one row:
         (1, 'resnet50', 0.8731, '2023-10-12T14:32:00Z')

    2. /home/user/mlops/sql_commands.log  
       • Two lines, each ending with '\n':
         Line 1: "CREATE TABLE experiment_runs (run_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, model TEXT, accuracy REAL, timestamp TEXT);\n"
         Line 2: "INSERT INTO experiment_runs (run_id, model, accuracy, timestamp) VALUES (1,'resnet50',0.8731,'2023-10-12T14:32:00Z');\n"

    3. /home/user/mlops/artifacts/run_1_summary.csv  
       • Two lines, each ending with '\n':
         Line 1: "run_id,model,accuracy,timestamp\n"
         Line 2: "1,resnet50,0.8731,2023-10-12T14:32:00Z\n"
